title: Citrusy Juicy Irish Beef Dumplings
description: A twist on traditional Irish beef stew, these dumplings add a burst of citrus flavor and a pop of color.
instructions: In a large pan, heat 2 tbsp oil over medium-high heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the beef mince and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the tomato paste, thyme, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the beef broth and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let cook, covered, for 10-15 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix together the flour and a splash of water to form a slurry.
Add the slurry to the pot and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes, until thickened.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the dumpling mixture (see below) into the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes, until cooked through.
Serve hot, garnished with additional orange and lemon zest, if desired.
To make the dumpling mixture, mix together the beef mince, orange zest, lemon zest, and pink food coloring (if using).
